openmeter/meterevent.purpose: Public API surface for listing metered usage events from ClickHouse; owns the Service interface, the Event domain type, ListEventsParams/ListEventsV2Params with hard constraints (32-day lookback, 100-event limit) and cursor-based pagination. The adapter/ and httphandler/ sub-packages implement and expose the service without touching Postgres/Ent.

patterns.name: Params.Validate() before delegation
patterns.description: Both ListEventsParams and ListEventsV2Params expose Validate() via errors.Join; callers (adapter, httphandler) must call Validate() before delegating to streaming.Connector — limits and filter constraints are enforced here, not at the adapter.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: SortBy-aware Cursor emission
patterns.description: Event.Cursor() switches on SortBy (Time/IngestedAt/StoredAt) to pick the pagination timestamp; StoreRowID is always the tiebreak.
patterns.example: case streaming.EventSortFieldIngestedAt: return pagination.NewCursor(e.IngestedAt, e.StoreRowID)

patterns.name: ValidationErrors as a per-event field
patterns.description: Individual event validation failures are attached to Event.ValidationErrors []error; the method still returns the result slice so callers inspect per-event errors without a blanket failure.
patterns.example: event.ValidationErrors = append(event.ValidationErrors, validationErr)

patterns.name: CustomerID filter: only $in supported
patterns.description: ListEventsV2Params.Validate() rejects a non-empty CustomerID filter that does not use .In — enforced in Validate(), not the adapter or handler.
patterns.example: if !p.CustomerID.IsEmpty() && p.CustomerID.In == nil { errs = append(errs, errors.New("customer id filter supports only in")) }

patterns.name: NextCursor emitted only on a full page
patterns.description: The adapter omits NextCursor when the result page is smaller than effectiveLimit, avoiding a redundant empty-result follow-up request.
patterns.example: if len(events) == effectiveLimit { result.NextCursor = &lastCursor }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, Event domain type (Cursor() + SortBy), ListEventsParams, ListEventsV2Params, MaximumFromDuration (32d), MaximumLimit (100). Source of truth for all validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a filter field needs a ValidateWithComplexity clause; adding a sort field needs both a SortBy constant and a Cursor() case — omitting either lets invalid input reach ClickHouse.

key_file_guides.role: Single service implementation: delegates to streaming.Connector, enriches with customer IDs (per-call cache), attaches ValidationErrors per event.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must not import openmeter/ent/db. Must not emit NextCursor on a partial page. Subject→customerID cache is per-call, not process-wide.

key_file_guides.role: All API↔domain conversions for v1 and v2 handlers, centralised.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Inlining conversions in event.go/event_v2.go violates the mapping.go convention and makes future API changes harder to audit.

key_file_guides.role: Composite Handler interface embedding per-endpoint sub-interfaces; wires sub-handlers for server registration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints need a sub-interface here AND registration in openmeter/server/router.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db in the adapter — this is a streaming-only (ClickHouse) package with no Postgres access
Returning a top-level error for individual event validation failures instead of attaching to Event.ValidationErrors
Emitting NextCursor when the result page is smaller than effectiveLimit
Skipping params.Validate() before delegating to streaming.Connector — hard limits bypass
Inlining API↔domain conversions in event.go or event_v2.go instead of mapping.go

decisions.decision: ValidationErrors are per-event fields, not top-level errors
decisions.rationale: Allows partial results with inline error annotations rather than failing the entire listing for one bad event.

decisions.decision: Hard time-window and count limits (32 days, 100 events) enforced in Validate()
decisions.rationale: Centralising limits in the domain params type makes them enforceable regardless of caller and unit-testable without adapters.

openmeter/meterexport.purpose: Streaming export of pre-aggregated synthetic meter events from ClickHouse; converts SUM/COUNT meter rows into RawEvents suitable for re-ingestion. The root package owns the Service interface, DataExportConfig, DataExportParams, and TargetMeterDescriptor; the service/ sub-package holds the dual-channel streaming implementation.

patterns.name: Config/Params Validate() before delegation
patterns.description: DataExportConfig and DataExportParams expose Validate() via errors.Join; the service calls Validate() at the start of every public method before touching ClickHouse.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Dual-channel streaming result
patterns.description: ExportSyntheticMeterData writes events to result chan<- streaming.RawEvent and errors to err chan<- error; channels are created by the caller and closed via defer only in the producing goroutine.
patterns.example: func (s *svc) ExportSyntheticMeterData(ctx, params DataExportParams, result chan<- streaming.RawEvent, err chan<- error) error

patterns.name: iter.Seq2 wrapper as the preferred caller API
patterns.description: ExportSyntheticMeterDataIter wraps the dual-channel method in iter.Seq2[streaming.RawEvent, error]; early break from the range loop cancels the producer context.
patterns.example: for event, err := range seq { if err != nil { handle(err) }; process(event) }

patterns.name: Custom JSON marshal/unmarshal for time.Location
patterns.description: DataExportConfig marshals ExportWindowTimeZone as an IANA name string; nil marshals to empty string and unmarshals back to nil.
patterns.example: func (c DataExportConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) { tzName := ""; if c.ExportWindowTimeZone != nil { tzName = c.ExportWindowTimeZone.String() }; ... }

patterns.name: Aggregation whitelist in validateAndGetMeter
patterns.description: Only SUM and COUNT are supported; validateAndGetMeter rejects all others before any export query, and must stay in sync with createEventFromMeterRow.
patterns.example: switch meter.Aggregation { case meter.MeterAggregationSum, meter.MeterAggregationCount: /* ok */; default: return error }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (GetTargetMeterDescriptor, ExportSyntheticMeterData, ExportSyntheticMeterDataIter), DataExportConfig, DataExportParams, TargetMeterDescriptor and their Validate()/JSON methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding an aggregation type here requires whitelisting it in service/syntheticdata.go validateAndGetMeter AND createEventFromMeterRow — missing either causes runtime rejection after successful validation.

key_file_guides.role: Windowed pagination loop driving the ClickHouse query and feeding the result channel.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never close resultCh/errCh outside funnel — channel ownership is exclusive via defer close; double-close panics.

key_file_guides.role: iter.Seq2 wrapper — the idiomatic range-based caller API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Early break must cancel the producer context to stop the funnel goroutine; otherwise the goroutine leaks on partial iteration.

key_file_guides.role: validateAndGetMeter rejects unsupported aggregations; createEventFromMeterRow constructs RawEvents from ClickHouse rows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The whitelist must stay in sync with createEventFromMeterRow — divergence causes silent data loss.

anti_patterns: Adding a new aggregation type to createEventFromMeterRow without whitelisting it in validateAndGetMeter
Closing resultCh or errCh outside the producing funnel goroutine — causes a double-close panic
Calling ExportSyntheticMeterData synchronously without draining both channels concurrently — deadlocks when buffers fill
Adding Ent/DB dependencies — depends only on streaming.Connector and meter.Service interfaces
Skipping the sync.Once guard for context-cancellation errors in errCh — causes duplicate ctx.Err() entries

decisions.decision: Dual-channel streaming rather than returning a slice
decisions.rationale: Exports can span many ClickHouse window pages; buffering all results would OOM for large ranges. Channels allow backpressure and early cancellation.

decisions.decision: Only SUM and COUNT aggregations supported
decisions.rationale: Pre-aggregated synthetic events are only reversible for additive (SUM) and countable (COUNT) functions; MAX/UNIQUE_COUNT lose information when windowed.

openmeter/namespace.purpose: Multi-tenancy infrastructure: Manager fans out CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace to all registered Handler implementations (ClickHouse streaming, Kafka ingest, Ledger). Handlers are registered dynamically before startup completes; the namespacedriver/ sub-package provides the NamespaceDecoder abstraction for HTTP-layer namespace resolution.

patterns.name: Handler registration before CreateDefaultNamespace
patterns.description: All Handler implementations must be registered via RegisterHandler before CreateDefaultNamespace; handlers registered later miss default-namespace provisioning.
patterns.example: manager.RegisterHandler(streamingConnector); manager.RegisterHandler(kafkaCollector); manager.CreateDefaultNamespace(ctx)

patterns.name: Fan-out with errors.Join (no short-circuit)
patterns.description: createNamespace/deleteNamespace iterate all handlers and accumulate errors with errors.Join; a failure in one handler does not stop others and there is no rollback.
patterns.example: for _, handler := range m.config.Handlers { if err := handler.CreateNamespace(ctx, name); err != nil { errs = append(errs, err) } }; return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: Default namespace protected from deletion
patterns.description: DeleteNamespace rejects deletion of the default namespace before any handler call.
patterns.example: if name == m.config.DefaultNamespace { return errors.New("cannot delete default namespace") }

patterns.name: RWMutex-guarded handler slice
patterns.description: RegisterHandler takes a write lock; createNamespace/deleteNamespace take a read lock, allowing registration to race safely with in-flight operations during startup.
patterns.example: m.mu.Lock(); defer m.mu.Unlock(); m.config.Handlers = append(m.config.Handlers, handler)

patterns.name: StaticNamespaceDecoder as a named string type
patterns.description: namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder is a named string type — cast a string literal, no struct init. Used in self-hosted single-tenant deployments.
patterns.example: var decoder namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder = namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder("default")

key_file_guides.role: Full Manager: RWMutex-guarded handler slice, RegisterHandler, Create/Delete/CreateDefaultNamespace, GetDefaultNamespace, IsManagementDisabled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No rollback on partial handler failure (TODO documented) — do not assume atomicity. NewManager and RegisterHandler reject nil handlers; callers must check the error.

key_file_guides.role: NamespaceDecoder interface (GetNamespace(r) (string, bool)) and StaticNamespaceDecoder named string type; dependency-free.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returning ("", true) silently scopes queries to an empty namespace; ("", false) signals the caller to reject the request. No domain imports.

anti_patterns: Registering a Handler after CreateDefaultNamespace is called — it will miss default namespace provisioning
Returning (true, "") from NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace — callers treat an empty namespace as valid and misscope queries
Importing domain packages (billing, customer, entitlement) in namespacedriver — must stay infrastructure-only
Assuming createNamespace is atomic — failures in one handler do not roll back others
Adding multi-tenant dynamic namespace resolution to StaticNamespaceDecoder — dynamic lookup belongs in a separate decoder

decisions.decision: Manager holds a RWMutex-guarded handler slice rather than a fixed constructor-time set
decisions.rationale: Handlers are registered dynamically at startup to support optional subsystems like Ledger that depend on credits.enabled; a fixed set would force all handlers to be wired unconditionally.

decisions.decision: StaticNamespaceDecoder is a named string type, not a struct
decisions.rationale: Self-hosted single-tenant deployments always route to one namespace; a plain named string avoids initialisation ceremony.

openmeter/portal.purpose: Issues and validates short-lived HS256 JWT portal tokens scoped to namespace, subject, and an optional meter slug allowlist; ListTokens/InvalidateToken are intentionally unimplemented stubs. The root package owns the Service interface and all input/output types with Validate(); adapter/, authenticator/, and httphandler/ provide the JWT implementation, Chi middleware, and HTTP handlers.

patterns.name: Input Validate() before service call
patterns.description: CreateTokenInput, ListTokensInput, InvalidateTokenInput each expose Validate() with errors.Join; adapters and httphandlers call Validate() before delegating to portal.Service.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: Token field set only in the CreateToken response path
patterns.description: PortalToken.Token is *string and nil in all read paths; the JWT string is assigned only after token creation, never inside the shared toAPIPortalToken mapping.
patterns.example: token, err := adapter.CreateToken(ctx, input); // then: token.Token = &jwtString

patterns.name: ListTokens/InvalidateToken return GenericNotImplementedError
patterns.description: These are intentionally unimplemented on the stateless JWT adapter (and the noop), returning NotImplementedError.
patterns.example: return pagination.Result[*PortalToken]{}, models.NewGenericNotImplementedError("list portal tokens")

patterns.name: Meter slug allowlist validation in httphandler, not portal.Service
patterns.description: CreateToken httphandler validates AllowedMeterSlugs against meter.Service before calling portal.Service; portal.Service has no dependency on meter.Service.
patterns.example: for _, slug := range slugs { _, err := meterSvc.GetMeterByIDOrSlug(ctx, ns, slug) }

patterns.name: AllowedMeterSlugs nil vs empty semantics
patterns.description: AllowedMeterSlugs *[]string: nil means all slugs allowed; non-nil empty means none allowed. Callers and the authenticator must distinguish these.
patterns.example: if input.AllowedMeterSlugs != nil { for _, slug := range *input.AllowedMeterSlugs { if slug == "" { errs = append(errs, ...) } } }

key_file_guides.role: Service (= PortalTokenService), PortalToken, PortalTokenClaims, CreateTokenInput, ListTokensInput, InvalidateTokenInput, and all Validate() methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AllowedMeterSlugs is *[]string — nil unrestricted, non-nil empty fully restricted; callers must not conflate the two cases.

key_file_guides.role: JWT-backed stateless adapter; Config.SharedSecret must be non-empty; strict JWT parse options (WithExpirationRequired, WithStrictDecoding, WithValidMethods, WithIssuer).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never store token state in a DB here — stateless by design; a DB-backed impl would be a separate adapter type.

key_file_guides.role: Chi middleware dispatching per OpenAPI security scheme; injects validated PortalTokenClaims subject into context via a typed key.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Auth failures must always be 401 via models.NewStatusProblem — never 200 or 500; the AllowedMeterSlugs check must happen even when the meterSlug route param is absent.

key_file_guides.role: API↔domain conversions; toAPIPortalToken must not set the Token field — that is set only in the CreateToken operation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token field leaking into non-create responses exposes JWT strings incorrectly.

anti_patterns: Relaxing JWT validation options (removing WithExpirationRequired, WithStrictDecoding, WithValidMethods) in the adapter
Setting PortalToken.Token inside toAPIPortalToken — assign only in the CreateToken response path
Adding DB persistence to the JWT adapter — stateless by design; a DB-backed impl is a separate adapter
Skipping AllowedMeterSlugs validation in httphandler when the meterSlug param is absent from the route
Returning 500 for auth failures in the authenticator middleware — must always be 401

decisions.decision: Stateless JWT tokens with no DB persistence for ListTokens/InvalidateToken
decisions.rationale: Portal tokens are short-lived and scoped; avoiding a DB table removes a write-path dependency and keeps issuance fast.

decisions.decision: Meter slug allowlist validation in httphandler, not portal.Service
decisions.rationale: portal.Service has no import dependency on meter.Service; cross-domain meter existence validation is a transport-boundary concern, preserving clean domain separation.

openmeter/progressmanager.purpose: Tracks progress of long-running async operations (e.g. ClickHouse export jobs) via a Redis-backed key-value store with TTL. Root package defines mirrored Service and Adapter interfaces; entity/ owns pure domain types; adapter/ provides Redis, noop, and mock implementations; httpdriver/ exposes a single GetProgress endpoint.

patterns.name: Validate() on every input and entity type
patterns.description: GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput, and Progress implement Validate() via errors.Join; the adapter calls Validate() before any Redis operation.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Namespaced Redis key format
patterns.description: Redis keys include namespace and operation ID to prevent multi-tenant collisions; the format lives in adapter/progress.go getKey() — do not change without a migration plan.
patterns.example: func getKey(input entity.GetProgressInput) string { return fmt.Sprintf("progress:%s:%s", input.Namespace, input.ID) }

patterns.name: redis.Nil mapped to GenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: Key-not-found from Redis is mapped to models.GenericNotFoundError so GenericErrorEncoder produces HTTP 404.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, redis.Nil) { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError("progress", input.ID) }

patterns.name: Noop UpsertProgress is a safe silent no-op
patterns.description: adapterNoop.UpsertProgress always returns nil; callers rely on this for test/disabled-feature wiring.
patterns.example: func (a *adapterNoop) UpsertProgress(_ context.Context, _ entity.UpsertProgressInput) error { return nil }

patterns.name: Service and Adapter interfaces mirror each other
patterns.description: Service embeds ProgressManagerService; Adapter embeds ProgressManagerAdapter — identical signatures so the Wire provider can inject Adapter as Service.
patterns.example: type Service interface { ProgressManagerService }; type Adapter interface { ProgressManagerAdapter }

key_file_guides.role: Outward-facing Service interface (GetProgress, UpsertProgress); must stay identical to Adapter signatures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a method to Service without adding it to Adapter (and vice versa) breaks the compile-time interface chain.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter interface (storage contract), deliberately mirroring Service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Diverging Service/Adapter signatures makes the Wire provider that injects Adapter as Service fail to compile.

key_file_guides.role: All domain value types (ProgressID, Progress, GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput) and their Validate(). Pure types only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding persistence logic or external imports here breaks entity/adapter separation and creates import cycles.

key_file_guides.role: Redis implementation, adapterNoop, and MockProgressManager (testify mock for Service); key format, TTL, and redis.Nil mapping live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing getKey() orphans all in-flight progress records; making adapterNoop.UpsertProgress return an error breaks callers relying on safe noop writes.

key_file_guides.role: Single GetProgress endpoint via httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs; namespace resolved via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not call the domain service from the decoder — decode maps request fields only; the operation func calls the service. Convert responses via progressToAPI().

anti_patterns: Storing progress keys without a namespace prefix — breaks multi-tenant isolation
Returning raw redis errors instead of models.GenericNotFoundError for redis.Nil — breaks HTTP 404 mapping
Changing the Redis key format in getKey() without a migration plan — orphans all in-flight progress records
Adding persistence, Redis, or HTTP logic to entity/ types — must be pure domain types only
Making adapterNoop.UpsertProgress return an error — callers rely on noop writes being safe no-ops

decisions.decision: Redis (not PostgreSQL/Ent) as the backing store
decisions.rationale: Progress records are ephemeral, TTL-scoped, and high-write; a Redis hash with TTL avoids Postgres table bloat and a cleanup job.

decisions.decision: Entity types in a separate entity/ sub-package
decisions.rationale: Keeps the root package as a thin interface layer; entity types can be imported by adapter/ without a circular import.
